title: When You Hear Something Repeated: - Ask:
subtitle: Question the Echo
description:
When you hear something repeated:
- Ask: "Is this backed by evidence or just popular belief?"
- Research: Find original sources and verify the information.
- Stay skeptical: Avoid accepting ideas solely because they’re widely shared.

Example: A common business trend might sound plausible, but digging deeper reveals no factual basis.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.
faq.answerHtml: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the claim
howTo.summary: Identify a repeated claim you accepted without verification. Write it down clearly, noting where you heard it.

howTo.title: Conduct a first-pass verification
howTo.summary: Set a 10-minute timer and search for the claim's origin. Look for credible sources and evaluate their quality.

howTo.title: Make a verdict
howTo.summary: Based on your findings, categorize the claim as supported, not supported, or inconclusive. Document your decision.

howTo.title: Share your findings
howTo.summary: Communicate your verdict with your team, providing a brief explanation and any relevant sources to support your conclusion.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your verification habit and its impact on decision-making. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/evidence-bot-for-slack/.
